what_it_does: A living requirements ledger and build spec for turning a bespoke coverage-audit harness into a first-class in-system operation, tracking goals, design decisions, proof-of-use-case results, blockers, and root issues.
observations: Dated 2026-06-30 by Tim with an explicit 'prove the use case first, note everything for later' approach
The goal is to retire the bespoke ops/ledger_coverage_audit.py urllib harness
Requirements are grouped into model/infra, role+input assembly, results+front, and a bigger interpretive-sweep rebuild
A reusable role roles/extraction_audit.py with structured output (findings list of objects, complete bool, kind_seen) is marked DONE + VERIFIED
A real-data gap was found: dials/anticipation.py module-level dict DIAL missing from extracted symbols
Outputs are stored as cas:// content with run:// pointers and op.run index records; flow chaining with run_reduce is proven
Two blockers are called out: B1 thinking-on timeouts force run_role(think=False), and B2 code:// resolver does not return ledger symbols
Root issues include extractor missing module-level constants/dicts, registry drift, stale provider binding, concurrency config not applied, and thinking-on timeouts
standouts: The audit already found a real systematic extractor gap (DIAL in anticipation.py)
Only run_role exposes think=False; other batch tools default to thinking-on and time out
code:// address resolution returns content only, not the ledger symbols the audit needs
Live model registry and running process are drifted from registered config; provider binding stays stale until manual /mcp reconnect
conventions: Uses Markdown task lists ([ ], [x]) for status tracking
Hierarchical sections with clear requirement categories
Root issues are numbered and labeled ROOT
Explicit DONE/VERIFIED markers for completed design items
Neutral ledger tone without ranking other files
concerns: A large portion of the work is still unchecked and blocked on root infrastructure fixes
Concurrency is configured to 32 but the running process is stale at 2; fixing requires a risky brain restart with co-residents to evict
Provider binding does not auto-reload on model swap, requiring manual reconnect
Thinking-on defaults cause timeouts across batch tools, so the scaled path depends on a single tool flag
Several items (prototype deletion, registry update, extractor fix) are pending approval or coordination
notes: This file is both a build plan and a session log: it records what has been proven, what still blocks a full coverage-audit integration, and which root fixes must happen first.
questions: What is the exact API shape of run_role, run_items, run_draft_items, run_reduce, and run_cascade?
What does the ledger symbol record look like for a code:// address today?
What is the code_archaeology projection and how does it pair files with symbols?
What is the 'other session' that is actively using the live brain?
What does 'F6 carry-forward' refer to in this context?
What is the registered schema/entry for chat-4b vs chat-4b-fp8?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a requirements ledger that grows as proof surfaces more, and that is exactly what the file does.
intention_signals: It is trying to plan and track the migration of a coverage audit into the system while using the audit itself to surface real extractor gaps and force underlying infrastructure fixes.
novelty: It functions as both a formal build spec and a dated runtime session journal, mixing requirements, verified proofs, blockers, and root-issue triage in one file.
contribution: A structured, timestamped ledger that captures the integration intent, a verified structured-output role design, concrete evidence of an extractor gap, and a prioritized set of root issues and blockers for the fused system to preserve.
summary_for_embedding: A 2026-06-30 build spec and requirements ledger for integrating a coverage audit role into the system, documenting verified role design, a real extractor gap finding, blockers, and root infrastructure issues.
intention_for_embedding: To guide the migration of the coverage audit from a bespoke harness into a first-class in-system operation while recording proof points and root fixes.
